title: Physics-informed monotone-constrained boosting
summary: The current best features encode physically ordered quantities like tyre age, degradation pressure, and finish-window deficit, but unconstrained trees can learn brittle reversals.
rationale: The current best features encode physically ordered quantities like tyre age, degradation pressure, and finish-window deficit, but unconstrained trees can learn brittle reversals. CatBoost supports monotone constraints, which is a good fit when higher window pressure or larger laps-beyond-finish should generally not reduce pit likelihood all else equal.
implementation_hint: Apply monotone increasing constraints to selected engineered features such as TyreLife_ToFinish_CompoundRatio, Compound_LapsBeyondFinishWindow, positive degradation pressure, and possibly laps-since-last-stop, while leaving context features unconstrained; compare against the same feature set without constraints.
expected_effect: May slightly lower train fit but improve public robustness by suppressing implausible local splits in sparse regimes.
risk: Wrong-sign constraints can hurt if a feature is only conditionally monotone; CatBoost monotone constraints are CPU-only, so training may slow.
